creator: vermaden@interia.pl
text:
"Yuri Pankov" <yuri.pankov@gmail.com> pisze:
 > /dev/random just isn't guaranteed to produce correct multibyte characters
 > (and I guess you are using multibyte locale). Try setting LC_CTYPE to
 > single byte locale:
 > > locale
 > LANG=3Den_US.UTF-8
 > LC_CTYPE=3D"en_US.UTF-8"
 > LC_COLLATE=3D"en_US.UTF-8"
 > LC_TIME=3D"en_US.UTF-8"
 > LC_NUMERIC=3D"en_US.UTF-8"
 > LC_MONETARY=3D"en_US.UTF-8"
 > LC_MESSAGES=3D"en_US.UTF-8"
 > LC_ALL=3D
 > > head -c 200 /dev/urandom | tr -c -d 'A-Za-z0-9'
 > tr: Illegal byte sequence
 > > head -c 200 /dev/urandom | env LC_CTYPE=3DC tr -c -d 'A-Za-z0-9'
 > myRUBav1EHfJrD0HCSuurHRPYmqMPzWErJhHRgp6ze0YisDoA4x
 
 Thanks you, locale was the problem, with
 LC_ALL/LC_CTYPE works as desired, You
 should close this PR as solved/unneded.
 
 Regards,
 vermaden
